behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is between manifest validation and module-loading side effects: the loader must reject invalid metadata and also prevent import/execution. That requires wiring manifest parser/validator + plugin discovery + module import path together. No real network/subprocess/OS primitive is required beyond local test fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugin directory with one plugin whose `manifest.json` omits a required field and whose entry module writes a sentinel file or mutates a shared flag at import time. Run the real loader against the directory. Assert: (1) loader reports validation rejection for that plugin, (2) sentinel side effect never occurs, proving no plugin code executed.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's `onLoad` hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: This is a cross-module ordering contract (manifest dependency graph -> load-order resolver -> hook invoker). A unit test of sort logic alone can miss invocation-order wiring bugs. No behavior text requires subprocess/network/real external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Set up plugins A and B in a temp directory where A depends on B. Each `onLoad` appends its name to a shared in-memory/event log file. Run loader and assert hook call order is `[B, A]`.

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection could be unit-tested, but behavior explicitly includes end-to-end effect "loads neither," which is a handoff between dependency analysis and load execution suppression. No stated need for real subprocess/network/OS-specific events.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins A and B with mutual dependencies. Instrument entry modules and `onLoad` hooks with side effects (log/sentinel). Run loader and assert: (1) cycle is reported, (2) neither plugin entry module nor `onLoad` executes.

behaviors.description: A plugin's `onLoad` hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: This is an execution-phase ordering boundary across module importer and lifecycle dispatcher. A pure unit test cannot prove real import side effects occur before hook invocation. Behavior does not require subprocess/network/external API.
behaviors.test_description: Create one plugin whose entry module sets `globalThis.pluginInit = 'imported'` and whose `onLoad` asserts that value exists, then records success. Run loader and assert no assertion failure plus recorded evidence that import side effect occurred before `onLoad`.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its `onLoad` hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: This is failure-propagation/isolation across dependency graph + hook execution + error handling. Real failure appears when modules interact: one plugin fails, dependents are skipped, independent plugins continue. No real HTTP/subprocess/AI dependence in behavior text, so System/Agentic are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins: `Fail` (throws in `onLoad`), `Dep` (depends on `Fail`), and `Ind` (independent). Run loader and assert: (1) error for `Fail` is reported, (2) `Dep` is not initialized, (3) `Ind` `onLoad` still runs.
